2026.macroRegime: Illustrative regime map: AI infrastructure capex remains the dominant secular trend, while energy, grid, semiconductors, defence, and cybersecurity act as bottleneck beneficiaries. Liquidity and geopolitics determine sizing.

themes.name: AI Infrastructure Bottlenecks
themes.thesis: Compute demand pulls forward demand for semiconductors, memory, networking, cooling, power, and data-centre construction. The core question is where scarcity converts into durable pricing power rather than cyclical overbuild.
themes.entryThesis: Add when capex guidance remains strong but valuation compresses with broad risk-off.
themes.exitThesis: Order books soften, utilization rolls over, or model efficiency improvements reduce hardware intensity faster than expected.
themes.riskCase: Supply response arrives faster than demand; peak multiple precedes peak shipments.

themes.name: Power, Grid & Critical Materials
themes.thesis: AI datacentres convert digital demand into physical bottlenecks: power availability, transformers, copper, cooling, and permitting. These areas are less glamorous than model labs but may have better supply/demand asymmetry.
themes.entryThesis: Prefer pullbacks driven by macro growth scares over evidence of actual demand destruction.
themes.exitThesis: Datacentre power demand forecasts are revised down or commodity supply becomes visibly unconstrained.
themes.riskCase: Global recession overwhelms structural demand; commodity beta dominates thesis.

themes.name: Autonomous Defence & Dual Use
themes.thesis: Modern warfare is shifting toward cheap autonomous systems, ISR, simulation, battlefield connectivity, and counter-drone defence. Public defence baskets provide liquid exposure while private markets capture the most interesting startup layer.
themes.entryThesis: Hold as structural hedge to geopolitical volatility and rising defence budgets.
themes.exitThesis: Budget growth decelerates or procurement reform fails to reach new entrants.
themes.riskCase: Large primes absorb budget while startups struggle to convert prototypes into procurement.

themes.name: Crypto Rails for Agentic Workflows
themes.thesis: Autonomous agents need payments, identity, reputation, and settlement rails. Crypto is not required for every AI workflow, but it is unusually well-suited to permissionless value transfer and machine-readable settlement.
themes.entryThesis: Track actual transaction volume, fee capture, and retention rather than narrative alone.
themes.exitThesis: Agents centralize around closed payment APIs and avoid crypto settlement.
themes.riskCase: Speculation outruns usage; token value capture remains indirect.

themes.name: Adversarial AI & Cybersecurity
themes.thesis: AI expands the attack surface: phishing, code exploits, identity fraud, bot activity, and agent governance failures. Security is one of the cleaner software exceptions to the view that incumbents capture most AI application value.
themes.entryThesis: Prefer assets with demonstrated enterprise distribution and AI-native product expansion.
themes.exitThesis: Security budgets flatten or AI-driven threat growth fails to translate into incremental spend.
themes.riskCase: Incumbent platforms bundle away specialist margins.
